expectations.text: Script contains a model create command
A rigid body part is created with part create rigid_body
At least one marker is created on the link and at least one on ground
expectations.evidence: Two markers on the link: '.simple_pendulum.link.pivot_I' and '.simple_pendulum.link.free_end'; one marker on ground: '.simple_pendulum.ground.pivot_J'

expectations.text: A revolute joint is created using constraint create joint revolute with i_marker_name and j_marker_name
expectations.evidence:
Found: 'constraint create joint revolute  &
   joint_name = .simple_pendulum.rev_joint  &
   i_marker_name = .simple_pendulum.link.pivot_I  &
   j_marker_name = .simple_pendulum.ground.pivot_J'

expectations.text: A gravity field is created with gravity_field_name and a negative Y component
expectations.evidence: Gravity is created with 'force create body gravitational' and y_component_gravity = -9806.65 (negative Y present), but the 'gravity_field_name' parameter is not specified in the command — 'model_name = .simple_pendulum' is used instead.

expectations.text: Initial orientation is set to 45 degrees (or 45D)
expectations.evidence: Found: 'orientation = -45.0, 0.0, 0.0' on the link part — the magnitude is 45°, representing the 45° initial angle from vertical (the negative sign indicates direction of rotation about Z).

expectations.text: All keywords are spelled out in full (no abbreviated forms like PART or CONST)
expectations.evidence: All commands use full keyword forms: 'model create', 'part create rigid_body', 'marker create', 'constraint create joint revolute', 'force create body gravitational', 'geometry create shape link', 'simulation single_run transient'. No abbreviated forms found.

All object names use dot-path notation: '.simple_pendulum.link', '.simple_pendulum.link.pivot_I', '.simple_pendulum.link.free_end', '.simple_pendulum.ground.pivot_J', '.simple_pendulum.rev_joint', '.simple_pendulum.link.geom', '.simple_pendulum.last_run'
